members.title:
Founder
Ranu Singh
members.details:
Ranu Singh is a Community Nutritionist and the Founder of Nutrition Punch, a platform dedicated to advancing community nutrition, public health awareness, and capacity building. She currently serves as a National Executive Committee Member of IAPEN India and is the Founder of the Community Nutrition Core Group – IAPEN India, contributing to the strengthening of community-focused nutrition initiatives at the national level. She previously served as the Secretary of the IAPEN India Lucknow Chapter, where she supported academic programming, professional engagement, and community outreach. She also serves as a Faculty for the Certified Diabetes Educator (CDE) Program under the Nutrition & Diabetes India Core Group of IAPEN India.
Beyond IAPEN India, Ranu leads the Community Nutrition and Public Health Committee of the PDA and has earlier served as the State Head (Uttar Pradesh) for the Global Nutrition Partnership Program of IAPEN India–ASPEN, contributing to interdisciplinary collaboration and capacity building.
Ranu is frequently invited as a guest faculty at universities, colleges, and medical universities, delivering academic and capacity-building sessions for postgraduate students of Community Medicine, paramedical and allied health sciences students, and health professionals. She is actively involved in training government functionaries, including ASHA workers, Anganwadi workers, District Programme Officers (DPOs), and Child Development Project Officers (CDPOs), and has organized Anganwadi-level training programs to support effective grassroots implementation of nutrition and public health interventions.
A committed community nutrition practitioner, Ranu works extensively with women of reproductive age in urban slums and rural communities, focusing on balanced diets, holistic lifestyle practices, sanitation, and hygiene to improve nutritional knowledge, health behaviours, and overall health outcomes. She is also deeply engaged in school and college-based nutrition education, promoting healthy eating habits, menstrual hygiene, and WASH (Water, Sanitation, and Hygiene) practices.
As part of her school health initiatives, Ranu conducts regular nutrition education sessions and health awareness camps in government and private schools, with a strong emphasis on rural areas and primary schools. Her interventions are age-appropriate, interactive, and practical, ensuring key messages are easily understood. In primary schools, she emphasizes healthy eating habits, basic hygiene, balanced diets, and anemia prevention through simple demonstrations and participatory activities. For older children and adolescents, her sessions address nutrition during growth, micronutrient deficiencies, physical activity, and the role of WASH in disease prevention. These efforts aim to promote sustained behaviour change, improved nutritional awareness, and better health outcomes, particularly in underserved rural communities.
Her community and school-based interventions focus on the prevention and management of anemia, diabetes, hypertension, obesity, and other lifestyle-related diseases through behaviour change communication, practical dietary modifications, and culturally appropriate community-based education. Key areas of work include interactive nutrition education, community-led WASH demonstrations, adolescent reproductive health and nutrition education, nutrition counselling for pregnant and lactating women, and the promotion of affordable, home-based dietary improvement strategies.
Academically, Ranu is a PhD Scholar with an MSc in Food and Nutrition. She is a Certified Diabetes Educator (IDF)and has completed specialized training in FODMAPs from Monash University, Australia. Her qualifications include multiple public health–focused postgraduate programs from the Public Health Foundation of India (PHFI) in Public Health and Nutrition and Management of Reproductive and Child Health. She also holds a Fellowship in Clinical Nutrition, is a Certified Maternal and Child Nutritionist, and has earned a Master Certificate in Programming for Infant and Young Child Feeding from UNICEF and Cornell University. Additionally, she has completed the MIYCAN program and specialized training in WASH, Humanitarian WASH Coordination, and Early Childhood Programming from UNICEF.
Further strengthening her expertise, Ranu has completed over 17 certificate courses from the European Society for Clinical Nutrition and Metabolism (ESPEN), reflecting her strong commitment to continuous professional development and evidence-based practice.
She is a co-author of three books, including Diabetes Mellitus: Nutrition, Functional Foods & Nutraceuticals, Sustainable Nutrition: A Guide for Dietitians to Health and Planetary Well-being, and Media’s Grip: Unravelling Media’s Influence on Society. She has authored over 150 articles in leading newspapers and magazines and has presented research papers at international platforms, including ESPEN Congresses in Lyon, Milan, and Prague.
In recognition of her sustained contributions to public health nutrition, Ranu has been felicitated by the Hon’ble Governor of Uttar Pradesh, Smt. Anandiben Patel, at Raj Bhavan. Her professional honours include the National Quality Award for Excellence in Public Health Nutrition (ICNC 2023, Chennai) and the State Level Quality Award (ICNC 2025, Varanasi), both conferred by IAPEN India.
Her professional affiliations include Life Membership in IAPEN India, the Indian Dietetic Association (IDA), Society for Renal Nutrition and Metabolism (SRNM), Nutrition Society of India (NSI), Indian Public Health Association (IPHA), and the Poshan Dhara Association (PDA). She is also an Associate Member of the American Diabetes Association, American Society of Nutrition, and International Society of Hypertension, and a member of the South Asian Association of Parenteral and Enteral Nutrition and ESPEN.

members.title:
Executive Member
Dr. Suneeta S. Chandorkar
members.details:
PhD
Associate Professor (Grade 3), Foods & Nutrition
The Maharaja Sayajirao University of Baroda, India
Education and Experience
Dr. Suneeta S. Chandorkar is an Associate Professor in the Department of Foods & Nutrition at The Maharaja Sayajirao University of Baroda, India, with over 27 years of experience in teaching, research, and policy engagement across Food Science and Public Health Nutrition. She has guided and mentored numerous postgraduate and doctoral scholars, contributing significantly to academic capacity building.
Awards
Recipient of multiple international and national awards for excellence in chrono nutrition, dietary sustainability, food labelling, metabolic health, and GIS-based nutrition research, including Young Investigator, First Prize/Excellent Abstract awards (IUNS, Apollo ICNU,IPHACON, ANH Academy) and the Young Scientist Award (NSI 2024). Recognised for research leadership and mentorship, including Mapathon Champion (IIT Bombay–FOSSEE), and recipient of the C. Gopalan-Srikantia Gold Medal for academic excellence (M.Sc., MSU Baroda).
Research
Dr. Chandorkar’s research spans food labelling and nutrient profiling, food safety,
sustainable food systems, GIS-based nutrition mapping, chrononutrition, DOHaD, and sports
nutrition. Her interdisciplinary work integrates laboratory analysis, population surveys, and
GIS tools to inform national and global nutrition policy, including contributions to FSSAI,
CODEX, WHO, and G20 (Brazil 2024).
Extension Activities
Dr. Chandorkar’s extension work focuses on capacity building, community nutrition
interventions, and knowledge translation, working closely with government agencies, NGOs,
academic institutions, and industry. She leads training programmes, policy dialogues, and
technology transfer initiatives that translate research evidence into actionable nutrition, food
safety, and sustainable food system practices across diverse populations.

members.details:

Dr. Arti S. Muley, an Assistant Professor at Symbiosis School of Culinary Arts and Nutritional Sciences, since 2018, holds a Ph.D. in Family & Community Sciences (Foods & Nutrition) from the Maharaja Sayajirao University of Baroda (2016). She brings 8 years of teaching experience.
Experience & Extension (Non-Academic)
Dr. Muley has 4 years of non-academic experience in high-impact nutrition and health projects. Her work includes a Research at Agharkar Research Institute, Pune on projects improving child nutrition with protein-rich supplements and developing strategies for non-communicable disorders in post-reproductive women. She served as a District Extender and Field Co-ordinator for the Gujarat Micronutrient Initiative Society (GMIS), contributing to the Vitamin-A Supplementation and various fortification/supplementation programs (Ready to Eat Food, Nutricandy, IFA tablets). She also worked as a Dietician at VLCC, providing personalized diet counseling.
Research & Publications
With 7 years of research experience, Dr. Muley has a prolific academic record, guiding M.Sc. dissertations and two completed Ph.D. studies (three more ongoing). She has secured research grants, including a significant RSSDI Research Grant of ₹5 lac. Her 41 publications (17 in 2024/2025 alone) span across prominent high impact journals. She also holds a Patent related to using alternative sweeteners in traditional Indian sweets.
Her research agenda is distinguished by its blend of clinical trials, public health outreach, and food science innovation, primarily focused within the field of Foods and Nutrition. Her major areas of research include: Public Health, Epidemiology, and Community Interventions; Clinical Nutrition and Diabetes Management; Micronutrient Status in Chronic Disease especially Vitamin-D; Food Science, Innovation, and Consumer Acceptability; Disease progression.
Awards & Recognition
Dr. Muley has earned several prestigious accolades, at national and international conferences for Oral/Poster Presentations. Her students have also achieved notable success, winning prizes at prestigious conferences. She has won a total of 15 distinct awards, divided between personal accolades and recognition achieved by her students. Her research has been featured in leading media outlets like the Hindustan Times and the Times of India.

members.title:
Executive Member
Dr. Vijayata Sengar
members.details:
She is working as Assistant Professor (CES) at the Dept. of Foods and has around 15 years of experience in teaching and research in addition to 8 years of experience as a diet counselor and a Diabetes educator.
Acdemically, Dr. Sengar holds a PhD degree in Foods and Nutrition with a Master’s degree in Nutrition from the Department of Foods and Nutrition, The Maharaja Sayajirao University of Baroda. She is a certified Diabetes Educator (Type 1 as well as Type 2) under National Dieabetes Educator Program, is a trained Nutrigenomics counselor and a Social and Behaviour Change Communicator.
She was invited as a researcher for 4 International interdisciplinary symposiums held at Oxford (UK), Kigali (Rwanda), Cambridge (UK) and London (UK) organized by Ryal Academy of medical Sciences, Royal Academy of Engineering along with The Academy of Medical Sciences, the British Academy and The Royal Society.
Dr. Sengar works comprehensively in the field of maternal and child health and nutrition including developing tools for dietary quality assessment, conducting studies on system strengthening within govt. programs as well as hospitals, capacity building of frontline workers, food and nutrition security, development of complementary food premixes as well as improving Take Home Ration consumption, early childhood development and nutrition to name a few areas of work in rural, urban and tribal areas. She is on the panel of external experts for various universities.
She has several publications to her credit including research articles, book chapters etc. and many newspaper articles as well as television video shows. She has presented at various national and international forums as a researcher, resource person or as an invited speaker. She has successfully completed several research and consultancy projects and currently is a part of two research projects with Government of Gujarat, Tribal Area sub plan for improving maternal and child health and nutrition as well as enhancing livelihoods for improving food and nutrition security.
She has more than 15 awards to her credit including the Young Scientist Award (Senior) in Community Nutrition conferred by the Nutrition Society of India at ICMR-NIN for her Doctoral research work entitled” Healthy Eating Index for Adolescents (HEIA) – Development and Evaluation” in 2013.
She has been the General Secretary, the Foods and Nutrition Alumni Association at the Maharaja Sayajirao University of Baroda for 3 consecutive terms since July, 2017. She has also served as Club Convener- Vadodara Club under Indian Dietetic Association Gujarat Chapter during 2014-2018 and as an Executive Committee member of Indian Dietetic Association Gujarat Chapter during 2010-2014.
She holds life memberships in Nutrition Society of India (NSI), Indian Dietetic Association (IDA), IAPEN India Association for Parenteral and Enteral Nutrition, Home Science Association of India (HSAI), Foods and Nutrition Alumni Association (MSUFNAA) etc.

Vartika Singhal
members.details:
Nutritionist | Founder & Chief Nutritionist – NutriAahar Nutrition Clinic
M.Sc Clinical Nutrition | 13+ Yrs Experience | Diabetes Educator | Certiﬁed Insulin Pump Trainer
Academic & Professional Profile
Vartika Singhal is a qualified Nutritionist with a strong academic foundation in science and a specialised focus on clinical and sports nutrition. Her professional journey reflects a conscious transition from an early academic pursuit in engineering sciences to nutrition, driven by her background as an athlete and a deep understanding of the physiological and practical challenges faced by individuals across health, disease, and high-performance settings.
She is the Founder and Chief Nutritionist of NUTRIAAHAR Nutrition Clinic, a centre dedicated to evidence-based, individualised nutritional care. Her work is rooted in scientific rigor, clinical assessment, and the application of nutrition as a therapeutic and performance-enhancing tool.
Academic Orientation & Methodology :
Vartika’s practice is guided by core principles of nutritional biochemistry, physiology, and clinical nutrition science. She adopts a structured, assessment-driven approach that includes:
- Comprehensive nutritional and clinical evaluation.
- Interpretation of biochemical and diagnostic parameters.
- Development of condition-specific and performance-specific nutrition protocols
- Integration of dietary therapy with lifestyle, training, and rehabilitation frameworks
Her methodology emphasises long-term metabolic health, functional outcomes, and translational nutrition bridging research evidence with clinical and athletic practice.
Areas of Specialisation :
- Medical Nutrition Therapy (MNT)
- Clinical and therapeutic nutrition
- Sports and performance nutrition
- Gut health and digestive disorders
- Adolescent nutrition and growth optimisation
- Injury recovery, rehabilitation nutrition, and micronutrient optimisation
- Evidence-based supplementation and anti-doping–aware nutrition practices
Professional Philosophy
Vartika’s professional philosophy is anchored in scientific integrity, ethical practice, and continuous academic development. She advocates for nutrition interventions that are data-driven, culturally appropriate, and sustainable, with a strong emphasis on interdisciplinary collaboration and patient-centred care.

With over four decades of unparalleled expertise, Dr. Subramania Iyer stands as a titan in the realm of surgical innovation and excellence. Having meticulously performed over 40,000+ major surgical procedures and more than 6,000+ free flap surgeries, his mastery in the field is undisputed.
Dr. Iyer's accolades include pioneering the first-hand transplants in the country and leading the centre with largest number of composite tissue allotransplants globally, a testament to his groundbreaking contributions.
Dr. Iyer's special interests lie in head and neck cancer surgery, secondary reconstruction, laryngeal reinnervation, and cutting-edge techniques like laser and robotic surgery, where he continues to push the boundaries of medical advancement.
His contributions in field of medical training are pathbreaking with establishing the first three-year structured training programme in head and neck surgical oncology and Indias first and only masters training programme in deglutology and swallowing therapy.

Speciality:

Dr. Krishnakumar currently serves as Professor at the Department of Head and Surgery and Oncology at Amrita Institute of Medical Sciences (Amrita Hospital). He did his MBBS at T. D. Medical College, Alappuzha and obtained MS (ENT) from B.J. Medical College Pune in January 2002. During May 2002 - August 2005, he worked as a consultant ENT surgeon at KVM Hospital, Cherthala. Later, he joined Amrita Institute of Medical Sciences to obtain his MCh (Head and Neck Surgical Oncology) and successfully completed the same in June 2008.
In July 2008, he joined Roswell Park Cancer Institute, Buffalo, New York for a two-year fellowship program in Head and Neck Surgical Oncology. During the first year of the fellowship program, Dr. Krishnakumar was actively involved in research projects on the development of newer photosensitizers for photodynamic therapy (PDT) for head and neck tumors.
During the second year, he was exposed to the entire clinical aspects covering ablative and reconstructive head and neck surgery with exposure to endoscopic skull base surgery. After completing the fellowship program, he joined Amrita Hospital in August 2010 as Associate Professor.Having over 17 years of clinical experience and 10 years of academic experience, he has presented and published papers in national and international forums. Won the Young Investigator Award, at the Indian Co-operative Oncology network.
He has more than 40 publications in international journals. He is the Regional Editor (Asia Pacific) of the Journal of Craniomaxillofacial Trauma and Reconstruction (AOCMF) and is a journal reviewer of more than 10 international journals including The Laryngoscope, Clinical Otolaryngology, Head and Neck. Has written a book titled “Basic Concepts in Head and Neck Surgery and Oncology”.

Research Specialization :
(Major scientific fields of interest): Dr Manjari Tripathi is a Professor of Neurology at AIIMS, New Delhi, with specialization in epilepsy.
She has over 20 years of experience in managing drug-refractory epilepsy and runs the epilepsy monitoring unit at AIIMS, New Delhi. Besides clinical research, Dr Tripathi has been engaged in cutting edge basic epilepsy research, particularly those involving next generation sequencing-based methodologies, to understand the mechanisms of drug resistance and epileptogenesis in refractory epilepsies.
Dr Tripathi's work is focused on epilepsy mechanisms, therapies, care and management, and has authored and co-authored over 500 articles in national and international journals of repute on epilepsy and related areas.
As a part of collaborative effort Dr Tripathi is the Co-Principal Investigator of the Centre of Excellence for Epilepsy which grants her access to wet-lab with specialization in “multi-omics” approach for discovery of epileptogenic and drug resistance mechanisms in epilepsy. These include, whole-genome sequencing, mRNA/miRNA sequencing, and mass spectrometry-based lipidomics and proteomics.
